judgment.summary: Zscaler exhibits strong revenue growth and healthy free cash flow, but its valuation remains elevated and profitability is currently negative.
judgment.earningsRecap: The last reported quarter (ending 2026-01-31) saw EPS of $1.01, beating estimates by 12.6%. The next earnings date is scheduled for 2026-05-26.
positives: Revenue growth of 25.9% YoY indicates strong top-line expansion.
Free cash flow of $1.02B TTM demonstrates robust cash generation.
Gross margin of 76.6% is very healthy.
The mean analyst target of $224.27 suggests significant upside potential from the current price.
negatives: Forward P/E of 38.20x and PEG ratio of 1.88x suggest a high valuation relative to expected growth.
Operating margin of -6.2% and profit margin of -2.3% indicate the company is currently unprofitable on an operating basis.
Return on equity of -3.6% and return on assets of -1.4% show negative returns on capital.
Debt/Equity ratio of 85.00x is relatively high, although offset by substantial cash reserves.

news.paragraph: Zscaler shares have experienced a significant rebound, with recent headlines highlighting its strategic focus on enterprise AI security through initiatives like Project AI Guardian and partnerships with Google Cloud and global system integrators. This renewed investor interest, coupled with a general software sector recovery, suggests positive momentum. Traders may also be positioning ahead of a potential catalyst around May 26th, although the broader cybersecurity sector is facing some scrutiny.
